g.data-narration: Welcome to Searching Strings in Java! Learn to find characters and substrings efficiently.
String searching is everywhere - validating emails, parsing data, searching keywords. Let's master it!
Java uses zero-based indexing. The first character is at position 0, not 1. This is crucial!
g.text: H e l l o , A d v a n c e d J a v a !

g.data-narration: indexOf() returns the position of the first occurrence. It finds characters or substrings. Try it now!
The second parameter in indexOf() lets you start searching from a specific position. Very useful!
lastIndexOf() searches from the END of the string. It finds the LAST occurrence of a character or substring.
To find ALL occurrences, use indexOf() in a loop, updating the start position each time. Classic technique!
g.text: Loop with indexOf(pos) - update pos each find

g.data-narration: contains() is a simple boolean check - does the substring exist? Returns true or false. Simple!
g.text: Note: contains() internally uses indexOf() - returns true if index >= 0

g.data-narration: Perfect for validation! Check if a string starts or ends with specific text - like file extensions!
When a character or substring is NOT found, indexOf() returns -1. This is NOT null - it's a valid integer!
All these methods are CASE-SENSITIVE. For case-insensitive search, convert to lowercase or uppercase first.
Real-world example: validating email addresses. Check for '@' symbol using indexOf()!

svg.g:
🌐 Real-World: Email Validation
String email = "user@example.com"; int atPos = email.indexOf('@');
atPos = 4 ✓ Valid email!
u s e r @ e x a m p l e . c o m 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 ...
</g|
📋 Quick Reference - All Methods
Method Purpose Returns
indexOf() First occurrence int (or -1)
lastIndexOf() Last occurrence int (or -1)
contains() Boolean check boolean
startsWith()/endsWith() Prefix/Suffix boolean
